feuds:
Heather Gay: ongoing rift that continues to grow through the season
Angie Katsanevas: strained friendship potentially beyond repair over sharp tongue and shade
key_moments:
Angie gives hair assessments and notes Lisa's hair is 'sleek and shiny,' adding 'she's eating it up that I'm saying that' — a subtle dig wrapped in a compliment
Lisa declares she 'loved all my outfits this season' and implies the others 'dressed for a funeral,' asserting confidence and superiority in fashion
talking_points:
Address the gossip and leaked information swirling around her and how it has tested her friendships
Respond to Angie's backhanded compliments and the state of their friendship
Speak to the growing rift with Heather Gay and what caused it to deepen this season
Own her fashion choices and stand behind her aesthetic — she was clearly proud of her looks all season
Set the record straight on the hair products drama and any rumors she may have been in the middle of
confessional_draft: I have always shown up — for this group, for this show, and for myself — and I'm not going to apologize for being confident. When Angie says 'she's eating it up' like it's an insult, that tells you everything you need to know about where her head is at. My friendships are being tested, yes, but I know who I am, and I know who was actually there for me.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds: Angie Katsanevas: friendship strained by Angie's tendencies rubbing Mary the wrong way
key_moments:
Andy references Mary's gold mic from her church, and Mary confirms she has a second one — a lighthearted but characteristically grand Mary moment
Mary roasts Angie's camping RV outfit: 'Mary Cosby, why are you dressed like we're going to the office?' — delivering shade with a smile
Mary receives the top hair compliment from Angie: 'this is the best your hair's ever looked' — Mary accepts graciously
talking_points:
Discuss her support of son Robert Jr.'s sobriety journey and how that has shaped her perspective this season
Address navigating the loss of her estranged mother and what that grief process has looked like
Speak to the tension with Angie Katsanevas and which specific behaviors have been problematic
Lean into her unapologetic, larger-than-life presence — from the gold church mic to her fashion — as a strength, not a liability
Clarify where she stands with the rest of the group heading into the reunion
confessional_draft: I have been through things this season that most people couldn't even fathom — loss, faith, family — and I still showed up every single time, fully. When you come into my presence, there is a standard, and not everyone can meet it. Angie knows exactly what she did, and so do I.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds: Lisa Barlow: ongoing rift that continued to grow throughout Season 6
key_moments:
Whitney jokes about Heather's U-Haul missionary outfit — 'I was trying to look like a missionary and I don't think it like—' — suggesting a memorable, mocked fashion moment from the season
Angie delivers Heather the warmest hair compliment of the night: 'Long, sexy. It's made you look younger... I want to run my fingers through it' — Heather is visibly pleased
Described as having her 'friendship with Whitney as strong as ever' while her rift with Lisa continues — sets up her reunion positioning
talking_points:
Address the deepening rift with Lisa Barlow — what specifically pushed it past the point of repair this season
Celebrate her empty-nester chapter and what it means to be prioritizing herself for the first time
Stand behind her friendship with Whitney Rose as a genuine, unshakeable bond in contrast to other strained relationships
Respond to any fashion criticism with humor — she can own the missionary outfit moment
Be prepared to discuss her vision for her future, personally and within the group dynamic
confessional_draft: This season I finally started putting myself first, and honestly? It looks good on me. My friendship with Whitney is rock solid, and I'm not going to keep shrinking myself to make Lisa Barlow comfortable. I have done that for too long, and this reunion is where I finally say what I actually mean.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds:
Mary Cosby: friendship strained by Angie's tendencies and sharp behavior rubbing Mary the wrong way
Lisa Barlow: friendship potentially broken beyond repair over cutting shade and sharp tongue
key_moments:
Angie is asked to give a hair assessment of every woman — she delivers a carefully calibrated mix of genuine compliments and subtle shade, notably saying of Lisa 'she's eating it up that I'm saying that'
Mary calls out Angie's camping RV outfit: 'Why are you dressed like we're going to the office?' — Angie acknowledges it wasn't her best look
Angie admits about the RV outfit: 'it looked like a driver's suit... cargo pants for camping, but it wasn't my best look' — rare moment of self-awareness
talking_points:
Be ready to defend the hair assessment segment — was the Lisa comment a genuine compliment or a dig?
Address the fallout with Mary Cosby and which specific behaviors created the rift
Speak to the state of her friendship with Lisa Barlow and whether it can be salvaged
Connect her Greek citizenship pursuit to her identity and why her daughter's lack of enthusiasm hurt her
Own her sharp tongue — acknowledge it as both a strength and a liability in this group
confessional_draft: I said what I said, and I meant most of it as a compliment — but if people want to read into it, maybe they should ask themselves why. My friendship with Mary and my friendship with Lisa are both things I value, but I am not going to file down my edges just to make everyone comfortable. That's never been who I am.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

key_moments:
Angie compliments Meredith's hair as 'really luscious... very sophisticated and elegant and timeless' — Meredith receives one of the more straightforwardly flattering assessments of the night
Referenced in producer context as enlisting Seth as a business advisor for her caviar lounge transformation — a low-drama but character-defining arc this season
talking_points:
Talk about the caviar lounge transformation at her Park City store and what that evolution means for her brand
Address the dynamic of working with Seth as an advisor — charming but complicated
Establish her position in the group's drama this season — observer or participant?
Speak to which friendships felt most stable for her this season
Be prepared to weigh in on the Lisa/Angie and Angie/Mary conflicts from her vantage point
confessional_draft: I built everything I have from the ground up, and this season was about taking it to the next level — which sounds simple until your husband becomes your business partner. I love Seth, but there is a reason people say don't mix business with marriage. As for everything going on with the other women, I see more than people think I do.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

key_moments:
Bronwyn shares she had to buy a last-minute dress after a mishap getting to the reunion — now owns two of the same Marmar Haleim dress
Andy asks about her first BravoCon experience — Bronwyn describes it warmly, noting the fan experience and excitement around Mary in particular
Described as still finding her footing within the group after 'a fiery reunion' last season
talking_points:
Address how she has navigated her place within the group since her fiery Season 5 reunion debut
Speak to relocating her parents to Salt Lake City amid health scares and a difficult diagnosis — a deeply personal arc
Establish her point of view on the group's conflicts this season — where does she stand?
Show growth and self-awareness compared to her explosive first reunion appearance
Talk about BravoCon and connecting with the fanbase as a newer cast member still building her footing
confessional_draft: Last reunion, everyone had an opinion about who I was before I even had a chance to show them. This season, I've been dealing with real things — my parents, my family, things that actually matter — and I'm not here to play games. I know who I am, and I'm done shrinking to fit into someone else's version of this group.

key_moments:
Whitney jokes about her missionary outfit at Heather's U-Haul scene: 'I was trying to look like a missionary and I don't think it like—' before being cut off by laughter
Others pile on the missionary outfit joke — 'I killed a few men on the stage' and 'a few chickens' — Whitney takes it in stride with humor
Angie compliments Whitney's short, sleek hair as 'really beautiful' — one of the first and warmest assessments given
talking_points:
Address the financial fallout from her company and what rebuilding looks like for her and Justin
Own the missionary outfit moment with humor — she's already laughing at herself, lean into it
Celebrate her renewed friendship with Heather Gay as a cornerstone of her season
Talk about rediscovering joy and her fun-loving side after an extremely difficult year
Be prepared to speak to which group dynamics she observed and how she positioned herself amid the conflicts
confessional_draft: Listen, the missionary outfit was a choice — maybe not my best choice, but I was going through it, okay? This season has been about rebuilding, about finding myself again after watching something I built fall apart. I'm still standing, I'm still smiling, and nobody is taking my joy this reunion.

Britani is not prominently featured in the pre-show small talk captured in this transcript segment

talking_points:
Be ready to discuss the exciting relationship announcement and what went wrong when old patterns returned

Respond to whichever cast member directed 'umbrage' at her — stand her ground without being defensive

confessional_draft: I came into this season with my heart open, and yes, things didn't go exactly as I planned — but that's life, and I'm not going to pretend otherwise just to protect an image. What I will not do is sit here and let someone come for me when I have done nothing but show up as my real, honest self. That's my standard, and I'm keeping it.
